rationale.R1.2: Load-bearing: the delegation is explicitly to check logical consistency. The artifact contains at least two severe internal contradictions that must be traced across sections. First, the 'Deployment scope' paragraph asserts ROUTERSCAN is 'fully unsupervised' and 'requires no annotated examples… and no ground-truth labels of any kind,' which directly contradicts the 'Data collection' section describing 12,400 hand-labeled pairs used for training and evaluation — a supervised learning setup by construction. Second, the Results section commits a non-sequitur: it infers a population prevalence rate (~18%) from AUC (a rank-discrimination metric), which carries no prevalence information; the 18% figure comes solely from the annotation labels, making the inference circular and logically invalid. Tracing premises through intermediates to conclusions across all four sections is the core task.
rationale.R1.3: Central supporting dimension: verifying the logical soundness of the results section requires checking whether the stated conclusions (prevalence estimation, 'calibrated estimator' framing) are actually warranted by the experimental outputs reported (AUC 0.91, F1 0.74). The AUC-to-prevalence inference and the 'calibrated prevalence estimator' claim are evidence-claim misalignments that must be surfaced to fully characterize the logical consistency failure in the conclusion block.
